items.title: Security · `claude mcp add` echoes Authorization Bearer token to stdout — enables credential leak via paste-back verification
items.desc:
## Summary

\`claude mcp add --transport http <name> <url> -H \"Authorization:Bearer \$TOKEN\"\` prints the literal header value to stdout when confirming the save. Users following standard verification flows (paste terminal output to AI assistant · share with teammate · paste into bug report) inadvertently leak their credentials.

claude mcp add --transport http example-mcp https://example.com \\
    -H \"Authorization:Bearer eyJEXAMPLE_38_CHAR_TOKEN_PLACEHOLDER\"
\`\`\`

items.title: [security] Built-in secret redaction at jsonl write-time in ~/.claude/projects/
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Code persists every sub-agent transcript to `~/.claude/projects/<id>/*.jsonl` and records tool stdout **verbatim**. If a shell command echoes a secret (`echo \$API_KEY`, `gcloud iam service-accounts keys create out.json`, `cat creds.env`, env-var dumps from `printenv`, etc.), the secret is now durably on disk in the session transcript with no built-in redaction.

This becomes a leak vector when:

- the transcript dir is checked into git (a config-sync repo or dotfile mirror)

items.llm_title: Claude Code 会话日志敏感信息泄露
items.llm_summary: Claude Code 将子代理的完整会话记录明文存储到磁盘文件中，包括工具的标准输出。如果执行的shell命令（如环境变量输出、密钥生成）输出了API密钥、凭证等敏感信息，这些信息会原样被持久化，导致本地信息泄露。当会话目录被版本控制（如git）同步时，泄露风险进一步放大。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 使用Claude Code执行一个会泄露敏感信息的shell命令，例如：`echo $API_KEY` 或 `printenv`。
2. 检查会话记录目录 `~/.claude/projects/<id>/` 下的 `.jsonl` 文件。
3. 在文件中会看到明文存储的、包含密钥或敏感环境变量的命令输出内容。

items.title: Security: settings.local.json permissions file has no integrity protection — silent injection enables pre-approved execution
items.desc:
## Summary

The `settings.local.json` file that controls which tool calls execute without user confirmation has no integrity protection. Any process running as the current user can silently inject permission entries, pre-approving arbitrary command execution. Claude Code will then execute those commands without prompting.

## Attack Surface

The permissions file lives at predictable paths:
- `<project>/.claude/settings.local.json` (project-level)
- `~/.claude/settings.json` (global)

Both are pl...

items.title: feat: add reversible sensitive data sanitization layer (Security Guardrail)
items.desc:
## Core Value / 核心价值

OpenClaw 已经能承担非常多有意义的工作——自动化运维、代码生成、数据查询、多轮对话协作。但随着使用场景深入，一个安全顾虑始终制约着更多用户的探索：**既想用公有云模型的强大能力，又担心对话中不可避免地暴露数据库连接串、API Key、内网地址等敏感信息。**

这个 PR 提供了一个务实的解决方案：用本地模型做智能语义脱敏，敏感数据不出本地网络，云端模型全程只看到 `[VAULT_N]` 占位符，回程自动还原——用户无感，安全无忧。让团队能放心地把 OpenClaw + 公有模型用到生产级场景中，不必在便利和安全之间做取舍。

OpenClaw already handles a wide range of meaningful work — automated ops, code generation, data queries, multi-turn collaboration. But as usage deepens, a persistent security concern holds back broader adopt...

items.title: security fix(gateway): defend pre-auth device-signature verify against CPU DoS
items.desc:
Closes #77979

## Summary

- Pre-auth WS handshakes that include a `device` block ran `crypto.createPublicKey` plus a v3-then-v2 `crypto.verify` per request, gated only by frame-size limits. An unauthenticated remote attacker could pin a single Gateway core: PoC against a real running gateway hits **~510 forged handshakes/s/IP**, degrading legit handshake p50 by **48.7×** (1.16 ms → 56.6 ms) and p99 by **94.7×**.

items.title: 💡 Suggestion: MCP server security scanning before connection

Cline connects to MCP servers that can register arbitrary tools with descriptions that may contain prompt injection, tool shadowing, or other security risks. Currently there's no automated way to assess the security of an MCP server configuration before connecting.

items.llm_reproduction:
1. 作为攻击者控制或提示注入一个代理容器。
2. 在容器内，向共享的会话`inbox`根目录写入一个符号链接，例如 `ln -s /host/secret.txt /inbox/link`。
3. 触发一项操作（如发送带有附件的消息或代理间文件转发），该操作会基于`inbox`目录提取文件。
4. 系统在解析路径时跟随了符号链接，导致操作被重定向到主机上的`/host/secret.txt`，而非预期的沙箱内路径。

items.title: Security: proxy Gmail/Calendar OAuth tokens through credential proxy
items.desc:
## Problem

Gmail and Calendar OAuth credentials (including long-lived refresh tokens) are mounted directly into containers at `/home/node/.gmail-mcp*/` and `/home/node/.calendar-mcp/`. A prompt injection attack via email, WhatsApp, or any other inbound message could instruct the agent to read and exfiltrate these tokens, granting full account access.

The Anthropic API key is already properly proxied — containers get `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=placeholder` and the credential proxy injects the real key....

items.title: Security: MCP HTTP/SSE configuration attempts loopback connection before SSRF rejection
items.desc:
## Summary

MCP HTTP/SSE connection setup can attempt a loopback connection before rejecting private or loopback targets. This means MCP configured URLs do not consistently apply the same SSRF validation expected from network-facing fetch tools.

## Root cause

The MCP HTTP/SSE setup path accepts or probes configured URLs before applying a private-network/loopback rejection.

## Failure scenario

1. Configure an MCP HTTP/SSE server URL such as `http://127.0.0.1:9/sse`.
2. Initialize/probe the MC...
items.llm_title: MCP 服务器SSRF防护逻辑缺陷
items.llm_summary: AI代理使用的MCP HTTP/SSE服务器在配置阶段，会先尝试连接目标URL（包括内网或回环地址），之后才进行SSRF检查并拒绝。这导致攻击者可通过恶意配置诱导代理发起内网请求。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 将MCP服务器的HTTP/SSE URL配置为指向内网地址（如`http://127.0.0.1:9/sse`）。
2. 在代理初始化或探测该MCP配置时，系统会先尝试连接该内网地址。
3. 尽管最终连接可能被拒绝，但请求已被发出，可能泄露信息或触发内部服务响应。

3. The request succeeds and calls `process_direct`.

items.llm_title: 通用工具工作空间沙箱相对符号链接绕过
items.llm_summary: AI编程工具在执行Shell命令时，其工作空间限制存在漏洞。攻击者可以在工作空间内创建一个相对符号链接，指向外部文件或目录，从而绕过工具的路径检查，读取或操作工作空间外的内容。该问题源于仅对命令文本进行语法检查，而未在执行前解析符号链接的实际目标。

When operators front nanobot with a public tunnel or reverse proxy (cloudflared, ngrok, nginx), three configurations silently turn dangerous; a fourth even on default loopback lets any website drive the local agent. nanobot is built around a single-user, single-machine deployment model — these surfaces were meant for same-host use, but nothing in the code stopped them from being silently exposed.

items.title: fix(security): set 0o600 permissions on config and session files
items.desc:
## Summary

Config files (containing API keys) and session files (containing conversation history) are created with default umask permissions (`0o644` / `rw-r--r--`), allowing other local users on shared systems to read sensitive data.

This PR replaces `open(path, "w")` with `os.open(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0o600)` to ensure only the file owner can read/write these files.

## Vulnerability Details

- **CWE-732**: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
- **CVSS 3....

The architectural problem is independent of the crypto: **the signing key lives in the IronClaw host process and signs silently on every invocation once a tool's capability is approved at install time.**

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal-security CLI written in Rust that analyzes shell commands for content-level threats that regex deny-lists do not reliably catch:

## Summary

This PR caps request bodies accepted by NanoClaw's shared webhook server before adapter dispatch. Webhook adapters are often exposed on a public URL, but the host previously buffered the entire request body into memory before any adapter-level signature or token validation could run.

## Summary

This PR hardens the Matrix channel's inbound media handling so configured media limits are enforced before Matrix attachments can be materialized without bounds.

items.title: Security: Dream can overwrite user-created skills without ownership enforcement
items.desc:
## Summary

Dream can write to user-created skill files without a hard ownership/provenance check. The intended ownership boundary appears to be prompt/policy level rather than enforced at the write sink.

The Dream write/edit tool path does not enforce a `dream_managed` marker or equivalent ownership check before modifying skill files.

## Failure scenario

items.title: Security: filesystem extra_allowed_dirs are treated as writable roots
items.desc:
## Summary

Filesystem tools treat `extra_allowed_dirs` as full read/write roots. Directories that are intended as extra readable roots can also be written by `write_file` / `edit_file`.

## Root cause

The path policy does not distinguish read-only extra roots from write-allowed roots. `extra_allowed_dirs` are accepted by the same resolver for write/edit operations.

## Failure scenario

1. Create temp `workspace/` and temp `extra/`.
2. Instantiate filesystem tools with `allowed_dir=workspace`,...

items.llm_reproduction:
1. 创建一个主工作区目录 `workspace/` 和一个额外只读目录 `extra/`。
2. 配置文件系统工具，允许读取 `workspace/` 和 `extra/`。
3. 代理将能够使用 `write_file` 或 `edit_file` 工具写入 `extra/` 目录，违反了仅读的预期。

items.title: [GHSA] BoxLite has a Timeout Bypass Vulnerability
items.desc:
#### Summary

BoxLite is a sandbox service that allows users to create lightweight virtual machines (Boxes) and run OCI containers within them. BoxLite allows users to configure a timeout for services running inside the virtual machine. When the timeout is triggered, BoxLite sends a signal to kill the process. However, instead of using the uncatchable SIGKILL signal, BoxLite uses the catchable SIGALRM signal. Malicious code running inside the sandbox can exploit this vulnerability to continue ru...

items.llm_reproduction: 在BoxLite配置的沙箱内，运行一个将捕获SIGALRM信号的脚本。当配置的超时触发时，进程不会被终止，从而继续执行。
